title: TNF Codebase Merkle Tree Schema
description: Schema for the Merkle tree data structure that ensures integrity across all 15,707 nodes in the TNF codebase.

merkle_root.description: The root hash of the entire codebase Merkle tree. This is the single source of truth for codebase integrity. Changes to any leaf node will change this root hash.

merkle_timestamp.description: ISO 8601 timestamp of when the Merkle tree was last computed.

merkle_algorithm.description: The hash algorithm used to construct the Merkle tree. Currently fixed to sha256.

tree_version.description: Semantic version of the Merkle tree schema.

tree_signature.description: Optional cryptographic signature of the merkle_root, signed by the Super Admin. Used for tamper-evident verification.

nodes.description: Array of all nodes in the codebase, each with their Merkle hash and sibling path.

id.description: The unique identifier of this node (e.g., PROTO_14, N1234, file_path).

merkle_hash.description: For leaf nodes: hash of the node's content. For intermediate nodes: hash of (left_child_hash || right_child_hash).

content_hash.description: Hash of the actual content (file text, protocol text). For leaf nodes, this is the hash of the file contents. For intermediate nodes, this is null.

merkle_level.description: 0 for leaf nodes (files, protocols). Increments by 1 for each level up the tree.

parent_id.description: ID of the parent node in the Merkle tree (null for root).

merkle_siblings.description: Ordered list of sibling hashes at each level going from this node up to the root. Used for verification.

node_type.description: Type of node in the Merkle tree.

children_count.description: Number of child nodes (for intermediate nodes).
